description: Capable of bending without breaking; able to adapt to different circumstances. 'Luwi' names the quality of adaptive strength—the willow that bends in wind, the mind that adjusts to change, resilience through yielding.
sound_symbolism: The word bends and flows. 'Lu' begins with liquid yielding, willingness to move. Then 'wi' winds smoothly, adapting to pressure.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'luwi lowi' (flexible vine). Related to 'mureo' (stable) as complementary quality—flexibility and stability together create resilience.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Flexible systems adapt to change—resilient communities can adjust to new conditions without breaking.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Flexibility of mind prevents suffering—rigid attachment breaks, while flexible acceptance bends and recovers.
